benchmark: Strategic Asset Allocation v1.2 (Growth, target CPI + 5%)
sources: NexSky's own view, informed by UBS, Syz, Pictet, J.P. Morgan and Luccai plus family-office peer data. Not a consensus average.
stance: Constructive but selective: risk-on equities with broadening away from US mega-cap, short duration, gold and quality as hedges, lean long the dollar.

scorecard.view: Strong EPS and AI capex, offset by concentration and rich valuation. Below UBS (Attractive). Broaden, do not add to mega-cap.
ECB hawkishness supports banks and the euro, and the valuation gap is wide. More constructive than UBS (Neutral).
BoJ normalisation, record earnings, better governance, Topix and Nikkei at highs. Aligned with UBS and Syz. Watch the yen squeeze.
China policy stability and earnings, though a strong dollar is a headwind. Take EM in equity form, underweight EM local and FX (the Pictet barbell).
ECB hawkish, heavy supply, sticky inflation. With Syz and Pictet at underweight. Prefer the short end.
Hawkish Fed and a flattening curve. The 5-year offers the best risk-reward. Belly for carry, reduce the long end. Against UBS Attractive.
Fiscal tail under Burnham, long gilts above 5%, a wider gilt-Bund spread. Avoid; the risk premium is rising.
Decent carry but spreads tight, around 80bps. Carry only.
Very tight spreads, around 300bps, late in the cycle. Not paid for the risk.
A strong dollar pressures local and FX, while hard-currency carry is acceptable. The Pictet barbell.
Central-bank demand and a fiscal and geopolitical hedge, against a near-term dollar and real-yield headwind. Hold through dips.
Hormuz reopened, so oil is soft, but the geopolitical optionality remains. Energy equity as a small tail hedge.
Copper is structural on AI, power and electrification. We like copper, with UBS.
A rate-differential reversal and the DXY breakout above 100.6. Against UBS's EUR/USD 1.20 bullish-euro call.
The SNB at 0% caps appreciation, with a haven bid on Hormuz risk. A three-way split, kept small and flagged.
High dispersion and range-bound rates reward macro, multi-strategy and low-net long-short.
The core growth engine, deployed through commitment pacing. A structural overweight; part of the SAA's ~30% privates.
Senior secured private credit for contractual income with downside protection through security and covenants; part of the ~30% privates allocation.
Logistics, data centres, residential, real-estate debt and energy-transition infrastructure. Selective.
Bitcoin is in a bear market, down 28% year to date, with weak momentum. Reduce beyond the strategic 2%.

peerComparison.note: OW overweight, N neutral, UW underweight, n/a not clearly published. Translated from each house's most recent published view; as-of date shown under each name. Some views are nuanced (UBS prefers short-duration bonds; BlackRock underweights long-dated IG). Sources: UBS House View, BlackRock Investment Institute, Morgan Stanley GIC, Citi Wealth, Goldman ISG, J.P. Morgan, Pictet.
